data.title: "Whales" recently suffered high-frequency trading failures, with a floating loss of $2.74 million on BTC and ETH holdings of $67.18 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Ember's monitoring, the "band whale" previously known for its 81% swing trading win rate has recently undergone a dramatic change in trading style, from steady swing trading to high-frequency long-short switching, but this strategy did not bring the expected returns, but instead fell into losses.
As of now, the whale seems to have suspended operations. On March 4, he spent 58.38 million USDT to buy 679 WBTC at an average price of $85,964, and spent 8.8 million USDT to buy 4,212 ETH at an average price of $2,090. However, this part of the position currently has a floating loss of about $2.74 million.

data.title: Trader Eugene: Bitcoin still has room to go down, or test the key psychological level of $66,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Eugene said that the overall trend of Bitcoin (BTC) is still biased downward, and there has not been enough market panic on the high time frame (HTF) to complete the deep cleansing of this cycle.
Eugene believes that although BTC has some support around $76,000 and may even form a double bottom in the $74,000-76,000 area, the probability is gradually decreasing. He pointed out that the upward trend or range of Bitcoin has been broken in all time frames, and $75,000 is the last important support.
In addition, he believes that the market will sooner or later test the psychological level of $66,000 in the mind of MicroStrategy founder Michael Saylor and observe the market's performance at this level. At the same time, due to the strong correlation between BTC and US stocks, he does not expect a reversal in the short term due to the remarks of Trump, Bessent or Fed Chairman Powell.
Eugene also said that if the US PPI data performs well tonight and pushes Bitcoin to rebound to a high of more than $80,000, he might consider shorting at high levels.

data.title: Nebraska enforces new crypto ATM law to prevent fraud, requiring sufficient warning disclosures
data.description:
Nebraska Governor Jim Pillen signed a new bill into law to combat crypto ATM fraud, aiming to 'build Nebraska into a cryptocurrency leader.'
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/346045/nebraska-enforces-new-crypto-atm-law-to-prevent-fraud-requiring-sufficient-warning-disclosures?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Nebraska implements new law requiring cryptocurrency ATMs to provide fraud warnings
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Nebraska has begun enforcing new cryptocurrency ATM regulations, requiring these devices to provide users with clear anti-fraud warnings before transactions. According to The Block, these warnings must be presented in written or electronic form and are intended to prevent users from making potentially fraudulent transactions without knowing it.
The regulations will take effect on March 13, 2025 and apply to all cryptocurrency ATMs operating in Nebraska. Operators must ensure that users read and confirm these warnings before transactions. The move is intended to raise public awareness of the potential risks of cryptocurrency transactions and reduce the occurrence of fraud.
(The Block)

data.title: Financial disclosures show six members of Trump’s cabinet hold Bitcoin assets
data.description:
Six of President Trump's 22 cabinet members listed Bitcoin wallets or indirectly held cryptocurrencies through other financial instruments in their January financial disclosures.
The six officials are:
Robert Kennedy Jr., Secretary of Health and Human Services
Russell Vought, Director of the Office of Management and Budget
Tulsi Gabbard, Director of National Intelligence
Pete Hegseth, Secretary of Defense
Scott Bessent, Secretary of the Treasury
Sean Duffy, Secretary of Transportation
The financial disclosure reflects the rising influence of crypto assets in the top ranks of the U.S. government, and the market is paying attention to the future cryptocurrency policy trends of the Trump administration.
(Fortune)
